approved_control.human_purpose: Repair the ylong_http HTTPS proxy SOTA benchmark so performance evidence is trustworthy rather than explained after the fact.
approved_control.primary_object: ylong_http HTTPS proxy benchmark harness, results, and evidence chain
approved_control.requested_transformation: Perform implementation-level benchmark trust repair: identify and remove or control Python TLS proxy send scheduling pollution, fixed client-order bias, small-sample outlier amplification, and mean-throughput aggregation distortion; then update tests, results, README/report evidence, and the SOTA claim decision.
non_goals: Do not merely rewrite README prose or figure captions.
Do not preserve a SOTA claim by statistical explanation of the old 0.890x cell.
Do not replace same-process libcurl-library evidence with curl CLI evidence.
Do not weaken protocol, TLS, mTLS, sync/async, or previous correctness requirements.
Do not use live external proxies, credentials, destructive operations, or tags without human_gate approval.
approved_control.how_we_know_purpose_was_met: A reviewer can rerun the benchmark and inspect run-local artifacts showing that the benchmark fixture, client scheduling, aggregation method, trace attribution, anomaly gates, tests, README/report text, and final SOTA verdict no longer accept proxy send scheduling noise, fixed order bias, low-repeat outliers, or mismatched aggregation as client performance evidence.
where_result_must_show_up: docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py or replacement benchmark fixture code
ylong_http_client/src/bin/https_proxy_bench.rs when benchmark binary changes are required
docs/benchmarks/test_run_https_proxy_bench.py and any new focused benchmark tests
docs/benchmarks/results/<new-run>/ with raw, summary, comparison, environment, and trace evidence
docs/figures/https_proxy_bench_performance.png and .pdf if README figure semantics change
approved_control.what_counts_as_done: The benchmark trust repair is done only when all required outcomes and result paths are directly evidenced: contamination audit completed; fixture or equivalent control implemented; client execution order bias controlled; paired aggregation, confidence intervals, and outlier/anomaly gates implemented; proxy send time and trace attribution included in result artifacts; tests pass; a new benchmark or controlled diagnostic matrix is run and compared with gen005; README/report evidence is updated; final Counterexample Gate passes; and the final report explicitly decides whether the SOTA claim is preserved, downgraded, or withdrawn under the repaired benchmark.

final_answer_format.medium: chat summary plus run-local JSON/evidence artifacts

source.quote: 以“修正 ylong_http HTTPS proxy SOTA benchmark 的测量可信度”为固定目标

source_requirements.required_action: Treat benchmark measurement credibility repair as the fixed controlled-run target.
completion_checks: The run does not drift into explaining the old result without repairing the benchmark.
The run does not treat README wording as a substitute for measurement credibility.

source.quote: 识别并消除 Python TLS proxy sendall 调度污染

source_requirements.required_action: Audit and eliminate or control Python TLS proxy sendall scheduling pollution in canonical benchmark evidence.
completion_checks: Audit identifies where proxy send time enters measured client performance.
Canonical benchmark either removes this fixture from the performance path or records and gates its influence.

source.quote: 设计并实现更稳定的 proxy/origin fixture 或等价控制机制

source_requirements.required_action: Choose and implement a stable proxy/origin fixture or equivalent controls that make benchmark results attributable to client behavior.
completion_checks: Design review records why the chosen fixture/control is adequate.
Implementation evidence shows stable request/response counts and bounded server-side send influence.

source_requirements.required_action: Implement client execution order control such as randomized order, seed logging, or A/B/B/A interleaving.
completion_checks: Result artifacts record execution order and seed or deterministic interleaving policy.
No canonical result is based on fixed ylong-then-libcurl order without an approved reason and counterexample review.

source_requirements.required_action: Implement paired aggregation and confidence/uncertainty reporting so one-sided low-latency outliers cannot dominate the claim.
completion_checks: Comparison output includes paired ratios or equivalent paired statistics.
Canonical claim uses an approved aggregation rule that includes uncertainty and downgrade behavior.
Ratio-of-mean throughput is not the sole SOTA gate unless justified and approved by counterexample review.

source_requirements.required_action: Add anomaly gates for proxy send time and related trace fields that can reject or downgrade polluted cells.
completion_checks: Trace output records server-side send time per client/cell.
A predeclared anomaly gate rejects or downgrades cells where server-side send timing explains the claimed client advantage or disadvantage.

source_requirements.required_action: Expose enough trace attribution fields to distinguish client work, server send timing, connection reuse, request counts, body bytes, errors, and TLS/proxy topology.
completion_checks: Trace fields are parsed and persisted.
Tests prove trace fields survive raw-output parsing and summary/comparison generation.
Final report uses trace fields to justify or reject benchmark cells.

source_requirements.required_action: Add or update tests that guard benchmark semantics, not only syntax.
completion_checks: Tests cover client order metadata, paired aggregation, CI/uncertainty fields, anomaly gate behavior, trace parsing, and README plot data source semantics.
Tests fail if the benchmark silently drops scenarios or reverts to a single-scenario plot.

source_requirements.required_action: Compare old gen005 evidence with repaired benchmark evidence and explain changes in measured results and claim validity.
completion_checks: Comparison identifies whether the old 0.890x cell remains, disappears, or is rejected by anomaly gates.
Comparison records whether old SOTA evidence is preserved, downgraded to historical evidence, or withdrawn.

source_requirements.required_action: Update README/report evidence chain so plotted figures, tables, metric definitions, and SOTA language match repaired benchmark evidence.
completion_checks: README does not cite superseded benchmark results as canonical.
Figures and tables point to the repaired benchmark result directory.
Claims distinguish canonical evidence from historical/counterexample runs.

source.quote: 是否保留 SOTA claim 的判定规则

source_requirements.required_action: Define and apply a final decision rule for preserving, downgrading, or withdrawing the SOTA claim after benchmark repair.
completion_checks: The final verdict cannot be inferred from old gen005 results alone.
The verdict states whether the repaired benchmark still meets the approved 20% SOTA threshold, with zero errors and no anomaly-gate failure.
If the repaired benchmark fails, README/report are downgraded honestly instead of preserving the SOTA claim.

source_requirements.required_action: Use human_gate for claim-changing/public actions and counterexample_gate for benchmark credibility and final completion.
completion_checks: Counterexample Gate challenges benchmark pollution, ordering bias, aggregation distortion, unsupported SOTA claims, and result-path decomposition.
Human approval is required before pushing claim-changing benchmark/report updates or tagging.

required_result_paths.statement: A canonical HTTPS proxy benchmark run produces client-comparable results that are not accepted when dominated by proxy send scheduling, fixed order bias, or low-repeat outliers. The repaired benchmark also has semantic tests that prevent reverting to polluted or unreadable evidence.
path_steps: execute ylong and libcurl using approved ordering policy
required_result_paths.completion_standard: The path is complete only when canonical results include trace-backed evidence that benchmark cells are attributable to client behavior and anomaly gates are applied before SOTA verdicts.

reject_if: All component tests pass but canonical results can still be dominated by proxy send scheduling.
Execution order remains fixed and unrecorded.
A low-repeat outlier can still determine the SOTA verdict.

required_result_paths.statement: The old gen005 SOTA evidence and the repaired benchmark evidence are compared, and the repository reaches an explicit keep/downgrade/withdraw SOTA verdict.
path_steps: run or load repaired benchmark evidence
required_result_paths.completion_standard: The path is complete only when the final SOTA claim status is tied to repaired benchmark evidence and old results are not misrepresented as canonical if they fail repaired gates.

reject_if: README/report still imply the old gen005 matrix is canonical when repaired evidence supersedes it.
The final verdict preserves SOTA without passing repaired benchmark gates.
The final verdict withdraws SOTA without explaining which repaired gate failed.

required_result_paths.statement: Independent Counterexample Gate and required human gates are applied before goal_achieved, push/tag, or final SOTA claim changes.
path_steps: request human approval for public/push/tag actions
finalize only after verifier permits goal_achieved
required_result_paths.completion_standard: The path is complete only when review evidence explicitly tries to disprove benchmark credibility and the final status does not bypass human-gated actions.

reject_if: The run claims completion without independent counterexample evidence.
Push/tag or claim-changing publication happens without required human approval.

facts.statement: The current benchmark fixture uses Python ssl.SSLSocket.sendall in LocalHttpsProxy and records proxy_response_send_us around it.
facts.why_needed: Design must know whether the current fixture can pollute client timing before choosing replacement or control mechanisms.

acceptable_evidence.description: Repository source lines showing LocalHttpsProxy sendall timing and trace recording.

facts.statement: The gen005 0.890x cell was not reproduced by targeted 20-repeat forward and reverse-order reruns; this indicates the old cell is not a stable implementation-slow-path result.
facts.why_needed: Design must distinguish implementation optimization work from benchmark measurement repair.

acceptable_evidence.description: Targeted rerun summaries showing different ratios and win counts.

facts.statement: A reverse-order diagnostic did not make libcurl preserve the 0.890x advantage, so fixed order alone is not a sufficient root-cause explanation, even though order still requires control.
facts.why_needed: Design must not select order randomization as the only repair if server-side send pollution remains uncontrolled.

facts.statement: The current README figure and comparison flow use throughput ratios in a way that can amplify low-latency outliers when sample size is small.
facts.why_needed: Design must define aggregation and uncertainty before execution so runtime does not invent favorable statistics.

acceptable_evidence.description: Plot/comparison data source and current figure metric semantics.

findings: Non-blocking: F-outlier-not-stable and F-order-alone-not-primary rely on temporary /tmp diagnostic artifacts; pregoal/runtime must regenerate or import run-local evidence before final claims.
Non-blocking: OBS-gen005-proxy-send-correlation has a weak reference and must be persisted under the run evidence directory before any design or final audit cites it.
Non-blocking: fixture choice, ordering policy, and canonical aggregation rule are intentionally deferred to design and must be explicit before runtime implementation.

required_outcomes.statement: Existing benchmark contamination sources are audited and classified before implementation.
required_outcomes.completion_claim: The run identifies which old benchmark signals are polluted, stable, or inconclusive and what must be controlled before making client-performance claims.

required_evidence.description: Audit report with source refs, rerun summaries, and candidate pollution sources.
required_evidence.evidence_claim: The report proves known benchmark pollution sources were identified before repair.

claim_scope.proves: The contamination model was inspected and recorded.

not_satisfied_by: Explaining the 0.890x cell without changing benchmark gates.
Only rerunning the same benchmark with more repeats.

counterexample_gate.completion_standard: Audit is complete only if it can identify how current benchmark evidence could still be false-positive or false-negative.
reject_if: The audit treats the old 0.890x as explained but leaves the same metric path accepted.
The audit omits proxy_response_send_us or client-order analysis.

required_outcomes.statement: Benchmark fixture or equivalent controls make canonical results attributable to client behavior.
required_outcomes.completion_claim: The benchmark fixture/control no longer lets Python proxy send scheduling dominate canonical performance cells without detection.

required_evidence.description: Implementation and tests for chosen stable fixture/control.
required_evidence.evidence_claim: The implemented fixture/control is tested and emits required trace fields.

claim_scope.proves: Fixture/control implementation behaves as specified in tests.

not_satisfied_by: Leaving Python sendall as canonical with no anomaly gate.
A fixture design note without executable tests.

counterexample_gate.completion_standard: Fixture control is complete only if polluted server-side send behavior is removed or detected and downgraded before claims.
reject_if: The fixture still accepts cells where proxy send time explains the advantage.
Tests do not exercise the anomaly-control path.

required_outcomes.statement: Client order and statistical aggregation are fair, paired, recorded, and resistant to small-sample outlier distortion.
required_outcomes.completion_claim: The benchmark records execution ordering and computes paired/uncertainty metrics that cannot silently convert one low-latency outlier into a SOTA result.

required_contexts: same topology paired ylong/libcurl benchmark cells

required_evidence.description: Tests and benchmark output proving order metadata, paired metrics, and uncertainty fields.
required_evidence.evidence_claim: The benchmark implements fair order and paired aggregation semantics.

applies_when: same topology paired ylong/libcurl benchmark cells
claim_scope.proves: Order and aggregation fields are generated and tested.

not_satisfied_by: More repeats with the same fixed order and old aggregation.
A plot-only change without CSV/test support.

counterexample_gate.completion_standard: Order and aggregation are complete only when final claims use the repaired paired evidence and downgrade rules.
reject_if: The final claim still depends on ratio-of-mean throughput alone.
Order metadata is missing or not checked by tests.

required_outcomes.statement: Trace attribution and proxy-send anomaly gates are implemented and applied before benchmark claims.
required_outcomes.completion_claim: Benchmark artifacts contain enough trace fields to reject or downgrade polluted cells.

forbidden_contexts: trace-only diagnostics not connected to verdict

required_evidence.description: Tests and results proving trace fields and anomaly gates are present and applied.
required_evidence.evidence_claim: Trace/anomaly gates are executable and affect verdict eligibility.

claim_scope.proves: Anomaly fields can reject/downgrade polluted cells.

not_satisfied_by: Recording trace fields but not using them in verdicts.
A manual explanation of proxy send time without an executable gate.

counterexample_gate.completion_standard: Trace/anomaly gates are complete only when polluted-cell rejection is automatic or explicitly predeclared and evidenced.
reject_if: A cell can pass SOTA while proxy send time explains the advantage.
Trace data is not persisted in run-local artifacts.

required_outcomes.statement: Benchmark semantic tests cover the repaired measurement model.
required_outcomes.completion_claim: The test suite guards ordering, aggregation, trace fields, anomaly gates, figure data, and comparison outputs.

required_evidence.description: Passing focused tests for repaired benchmark semantics.

claim_scope.proves: Focused tests pass for benchmark semantics.

not_satisfied_by: Tests that only assert output files exist.

counterexample_gate.completion_standard: Test matrix is complete only when tests fail against meaningful semantic regressions, not only syntax errors.
reject_if: Tests pass while order metadata, paired aggregation, or anomaly gates are absent.
Tests do not cover the old failure mode.

required_outcomes.statement: Old gen005 results are compared with repaired benchmark evidence.
required_outcomes.completion_claim: The run records how repaired benchmark evidence changes or confirms the old SOTA result.

required_evidence.description: New benchmark result and explicit comparison against gen005.
required_evidence.evidence_claim: Old and new benchmark evidence have been compared under repaired gates.

claim_scope.proves: The new result has been compared with old gen005 evidence.

not_satisfied_by: Only rerunning the outlier cell without the full canonical matrix or an approved scoped diagnostic.

counterexample_gate.completion_standard: Old/new comparison is complete only when the final claim explains how repaired gates affect old evidence.
reject_if: The comparison omits the old 0.890x cell.
The comparison ignores anomaly-gate outcomes.

required_outcomes.statement: README/report evidence and final SOTA claim match repaired benchmark evidence.
required_outcomes.completion_claim: Repository-facing performance text and final report honestly state whether SOTA is preserved, downgraded, or withdrawn.

required_evidence.description: README/report and figure artifacts updated to match repaired benchmark evidence.
required_evidence.evidence_claim: Repository-facing README/report/figure artifacts were changed to match repaired benchmark evidence.

claim_scope.proves: Documentation and final verdict were updated consistently.

required_evidence.description: Final SOTA verdict review tied to repaired benchmark evidence and final-report.json.
required_evidence.evidence_claim: The final SOTA verdict was reviewed against the repaired benchmark evidence and 20% threshold rule.

claim_scope.proves: Documentation and final verdict were updated consistently.

not_satisfied_by: Keeping SOTA wording when repaired gates fail.
Updating README without changing benchmark evidence.

counterexample_gate.completion_standard: README/report verdict is complete only when wording is backed by repaired benchmark evidence and no old result is falsely canonical.
reject_if: README still states a SOTA claim that repaired evidence does not support.
README hides a failed anomaly gate or below-threshold repaired result.

required_outcomes.statement: Counterexample and human gates are applied before final completion or public claim-changing actions.
required_outcomes.completion_claim: Independent review and human approval requirements prevent premature completion, push/tag, or public SOTA-claim changes.

required_evidence.evidence_claim: Independent review passed or identified unresolved blockers.

claim_scope.proves: The required independent challenge was performed.
does_not_prove: Human approval for push/tag was granted

required_evidence.description: Human approvals for push/tag or claim-changing publication, if such actions are performed.
required_evidence.evidence_claim: Required human gates were obtained before gated actions.

claim_scope.proves: Human approval existed for that action.

not_satisfied_by: Pushing or tagging without explicit human approval.

counterexample_gate.completion_standard: Gate outcome is complete only when independent review and required human approvals are evidenced before final claims/actions.
reject_if: Completion is claimed with only self-review.
A gated action occurs before approval.

no_offset.reason: A passing SOTA threshold cannot offset a failed anomaly gate.
README/report cleanup cannot offset missing benchmark repair.
More repeats cannot offset uncontrolled fixture pollution.
A passing old gen005 result cannot offset a failed repaired benchmark verdict.
Counterexample Gate evidence cannot offset missing tests or benchmark results.

counterexample_gate_contract.quality_standard: Independent reverse review must try to disprove that the repaired benchmark actually measures client performance rather than fixture scheduling, execution-order artifacts, aggregation artifacts, or README/report narrative.

minimum_reviewer.independence: The reviewer must be independent from the execution agent's own completion claim.

reject_if: A benchmark cell can still pass SOTA while proxy send time explains the measured advantage.
Fixed client order remains unrecorded or ungated.
The SOTA verdict can still be based on ratio-of-mean throughput alone under high variance.
All component tests can pass while a required result path still fails.
README/report preserves a SOTA claim unsupported by repaired benchmark evidence.
The run claims completion without explicit keep/downgrade/withdraw verdict for SOTA.
